expected_properties: If any input contains X or Z, mul_out should become X (unknown) or remain stable without causing simulation errors
additional_cases.goal: Check handling of unknown/invalid input values on mul_a and mul_b
notes: Contract does not define illegal input behavior; this case probes implementation robustness
stimulus_intent: Drive X (unknown) to mul_a while providing a valid value to mul_b
timing_assumptions: Observe mul_out over several clock cycles to see if it reflects the unknown input

expected_properties: The product output mul_out for (a,b) equals mul_out for (b,a) when both pairs are applied sequentially
additional_cases.goal: Validate the commutative property of unsigned multiplication
notes: Uses two input pairs that are permutations of each other
stimulus_intent: Apply (mul_a = A, mul_b = B), capture mul_out, then apply (mul_a = B, mul_b = A) and compare outputs
timing_assumptions: Allow sufficient clock cycles after each stimulus for mul_out to stabilize before comparison

expected_properties: Output eventually reflects the product of mul_a and mul_b within a conservative observation window
No reliance on fixed-cycle latency; stability is observed before asserting correctness
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Validate functional correctness of the multiplier for a random legal input pair
notes: Uses random stimulus within the defined input width
Observes mul_out until it stabilizes after the operation
stimulus_intent: Drive random values to mul_a and mul_b and monitor mul_out for correct product
timing_assumptions: Allow multiple clock cycles after stimulus before checking mul_out stability
